faqs.question: How does a solar water heater work?
faqs.answer: Solar water heaters use rooftop solar collectors to absorb heat from the sun and transfer it to your water supply. Most systems include a backup conventional heater for cloudy days, ensuring you always have hot water.

faqs.question: How much can I save with a solar water heater?
faqs.answer: Solar water heaters can reduce your water heating costs by 50–80%. The exact savings depend on your hot water usage, system size, and local sunlight conditions. Most systems pay for themselves within 5–7 years.

faqs.question: Are there tax credits for solar water heaters?
faqs.answer: Yes, federal tax credits are available for qualifying solar water heating systems. Additionally, California offers state incentives and local utility rebates. We can help you understand which incentives apply to your installation.
